title: Clinical Guidance for Group A Streptococcal Pharyngitis
license_notes: Use as government source support for strep testing, viral pharyngitis, and antibiotic stewardship. Avoid implying CDC endorses local discharge wording.
supports: A positive rapid antigen detection test or throat culture confirms group A strep pharyngitis.
Viral pharyngitis should not be treated with antibiotics.
Negative rapid antigen testing has pediatric-specific follow-up culture considerations.
